title: SpaceX launching 45 satellites to orbit early May 3: Watch it live
summary: SpaceX is set to launch 45 satellites to orbit from California early Sunday morning (May 3), with live coverage available. This mission highlights SpaceX's ongoing satellite deployment efforts.

title: New for Star Wars Day, grab this Lego Star Wars model of The Mandalorian's coolest ship and get the Darksaber for free
summary: Lego released a new UCS set of The Mandalorian's N-1 starfighter for Star Wars Day, with a free Darksaber model for a limited time.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 208 — Lander, Lander, Who's Got a Lander
summary: This Week In Space podcast episode 208 discusses the risks and realities of the new space race to the moon. The episode features a conversation with Space.com's Mike Wall.

title: From new releases to classic models, feel the force this Star Wars Day with the best Lego sets from the saga
summary: Lego is launching new Star Wars-themed sets for Star Wars Day, highlighting both new releases and classic models.

title: Galaxies, Artemis 2, space telescopes and stormtroopers: Here are the best photos from our staff
summary: The article highlights a collection of the best space-related photos from a team, featuring topics like Galaxies, Artemis 2, space telescopes, and stormtroopers.

title: See May's full 'Flower Moon' bloom in gorgeous photos from around the world
summary: The Flower Moon, the full moon in May, was captured in stunning photographs worldwide. It will be followed by a rare full Blue Moon on May 31.

title: 3 planets will put on a sky show in May — when and how to see them
summary: Venus, Jupiter, and Mercury will align in the sky in May, offering a rare three-planet display. The event will be accompanied by stunning moon pairings, providing a spectacular skywatching opportunity.

title: NASA Kennedy Center Director Announces Plans to Retire
summary: NASA's Kennedy Space Center Director Janet Petro announced her retirement after a career spanning over 15 years at the agency, including roles as center director and acting administrator. Petro, who joined NASA in 2007, previously held military and industry positions before her tenure at the space center.

title: NASA Artemis II Crew Rings Nasdaq Closing Bell
summary: NASA's Artemis II crew rang the Nasdaq Closing Bell to celebrate the mission, highlighting international collaboration with the Canadian Space Agency. The event marked a significant milestone for the Artemis program ahead of its planned lunar flyby mission.

title: Key Support Equipment Arrives at Kennedy for Roman Space Telescope
summary: NASA received key support equipment, including HEPA wall modules, for the Roman Space Telescope at Kennedy Space Center's Payload Hazardous Servicing Facility. The equipment will aid in launch processing for the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope.

title: NASA’s STORIE Mission to Tell Tale of Earth’s Ring Current
summary: NASA's STORIE Mission aims to study Earth's ring current, a doughnut-shaped region of trapped charged particles that influences space weather. The mission will help understand how Earth's magnetic field interacts with these particles.

title: Space Out This Summer with Variety of NASA STEM Activities
summary: NASA is offering a variety of STEM activities for summer, encouraging creativity, outdoor exploration, and future aspirations. The initiatives aim to engage the public in space-related learning and innovation.

title: Hubble Spots a Starry Spiral
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the spiral galaxy NGC 3137, located 53 million light-years away in the constellation Antlia. This galaxy provides astronomers with a prime opportunity to study the lifecycle of stars.

title: Cyclone Rains Spur Papua New Guinea Landslides
summary: Tropical Cyclone Maila caused a deadly landslide in Papua New Guinea's East New Britain due to heavy rains. The disaster highlights the region's vulnerability to extreme weather events.

title: NASA Invites Media to Ireland Artemis Accords Signing
summary: Ireland is set to sign the Artemis Accords during a ceremony at NASA Headquarters in Washington, hosted by NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman. The event will include participation from Irish and U.S. officials, including the Ambassador of Ireland to the U.S.

title: Odyssey Team Celebrates on a Global Map of Mars
summary: NASA's 2001 Mars Odyssey orbiter mission celebrated its 25th anniversary on April 15, 2026, with a team gathering and the unveiling of a giant global map of Mars created using Odyssey's THEMIS imagery.

title: What’s Up: May 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA
summary: NASA shares skywatching tips for May 2026, including meteor showers, a Moon-Venus conjunction, and a rare blue moon. The event highlights celestial events visible to the public.

title: SpaceX aims for mid-May Starship Flight 12 launch with revised trajectory
summary: SpaceX is targeting a mid-May launch for its 12th Starship flight, featuring a revised trajectory. The upcoming mission aims to build on previous test flights with improved performance.

title: Russia debuts new rocket with maiden Soyuz-5 launch
summary: Russia successfully conducted the maiden launch of its new Soyuz-5 rocket on Saturday. This marks a significant milestone for the country's space program.

title: Next NASA ISS astronaut, doctor discusses upcoming mission
summary: A doctor with experience in mass casualty events and overseas service has been selected as the next NASA astronaut for an upcoming ISS mission. The astronaut discussed preparations and expectations for the mission.

title: SpaceX launches Falcon Heavy on Wednesday after 18 month hiatus
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Falcon Heavy rocket after an 18-month hiatus. This marks a significant return for the heavy-lift vehicle.

title: Artemis III core stage assembly arrives at the Kennedy Space Center
summary: The core stage assembly for NASA's Artemis III mission has arrived at the Kennedy Space Center. This follows the recent Artemis II launch, which carried astronauts on a lunar flyby mission.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon Heavy returns, Atlas V and Ariane 6 to launch Amazon Leo satellites
summary: Falcon Heavy, Atlas V, and Ariane 6 are set to launch Amazon's Leo satellites this week from multiple locations. SpaceX's Falcon Heavy is making a return in this series of launches.

title: Curiosity Rover uncovers fresh organic clues on Ancient Mars
summary: NASA's Curiosity Rover has discovered new organic clues on Mars, advancing the search for ancient life beyond Earth. The findings were reported by NASASpaceFlight.com.

title: Progress MS-34 to launch from Baikonur for ISS cargo delivery
summary: Progress MS-34 is scheduled to launch from Baikonur to deliver cargo to the International Space Station (ISS), following the recent CRS NG-24 mission.

title: Starship Progress at the Cape with Hoping to Launch this year
summary: SpaceX is making progress on the Starship program at Cape Canaveral, aiming for a launch this year as the first flight of Block 3 approaches.

title: NASA and Axiom react to OIG Report on delays in Next-Generation Spacesuit Program
summary: NASA and Axiom Space have responded to a critical report from NASA's Office of Inspector General highlighting delays in the Next-Generation Spacesuit Program. The OIG report warns of potential setbacks in the development of new spacesuits for future missions.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years of achievements with a commemorative booklet highlighting 50 hallmark milestones. The publication reflects on the agency's contributions to space exploration and science over five decades.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space industry event involving a parachute test or experiment, referred to as 'The great parachute bake-out'.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The mission, launching in 2028, aims to search for signs of life while adhering to strict planetary protection protocols.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing space debris or satellites. This innovation aims to improve the safety and efficiency of space operations.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, held on June 8-9 in Paris, with an online preparatory session in April-May. The event aims to gather feedback on topics affecting the space sector.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal has been successfully received, marking a significant milestone for the project. The signal was captured at an optimal location, demonstrating the system's capability.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has created 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona observations. The mission revealed that solar wind structures in the inner corona travel 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Space Nuclear Execs Cheer the FY27 Budget Proposal
summary: NASA's FY27 budget proposal has been praised by space nuclear executives, with Administrator Isaacman highlighting it as a key step in the agency's evolution. The proposal reflects NASA's strong support for advancing space nuclear technology.

title: House Subcommittee Approves $24.4B NASA Budget
summary: The House Subcommittee has approved a $24.4 billion budget for NASA, rejecting the administration's FY27 budget request. This marks the first legislative step in formal opposition to the proposed spending plan.

title: European Space Firms Raised €1.4B of Private Capital in 2025
summary: European space firms secured €1.4 billion in private capital in 2025, reflecting growing investor confidence in the sector. This trend highlights the increasing comfort of private capital markets in supporting space industry startups.

title: BioOrbit Raises £9.8M Seed To Manufacture Cancer Drugs in Orbit
summary: BioOrbit has raised £9.8 million in seed funding to manufacture cancer drugs in orbit using microgravity. The company aims to reformulate drugs for self-administration by patients.

title: CATALYST Unveils its Sat Product for City Skylines
summary: CATALYST has introduced a new satellite product designed for urban planning and risk assessment. The product aims to help construction firms, insurers, and city authorities monitor developments and assess risks more effectively.

title: York Space Systems to Acquire ALL.SPACE for $355M
summary: York Space Systems is acquiring ALL.SPACE for $355 million to expand into high-growth markets like communications and multi-domain military applications. The deal aims to combine technologies to enhance York's capabilities in these sectors.

title: Orbital Data Center Competitive Landscape
summary: The orbital data center market is seeing competition among major players like SpaceX, Blue Origin, and Sophia Space, along with satellite manufacturers such as Apex and K2 Space.

title: DPhi’s Second Demo Brings Compute Space to Orbit
summary: Swiss startup DPhi Space successfully demonstrated in-orbit computing by running a large language model on its Clustergate-2 mission, analyzing an EO image without ground transmission. The mission, launched in March aboard a Momentus Vigoride 7 satellite, marks a new era in space-based AI processing.

title: Golden Dome Will Use COTS Parts, Space Execs Say
summary: Golden Dome satellite bus will utilize Commercial Off-The-Shelf (COTS) parts, according to space executives. Apex Space CEO Ian Cinnamon emphasized that the Golden Dome aims to be indistinguishable from commercial satellite buses.

title: Dems Slam Trump Over NSF Board Cuts
summary: Democrats criticize Trump's administration for proposed cuts to the National Science Foundation (NSF) board, calling it detrimental to science and innovation. The criticism comes as part of ongoing political debates over federal funding for research.

title: Loft to Build 10 EO Satellites for French Constellation With Magellium Artal Group
summary: Loft will build 10 Earth Observation (EO) satellites for a French constellation in partnership with Magellium Artal Group. This marks a significant next-generation EO project in France.

title: Orbital Warfare Systems Top Priority for Space Chief Nominee Lt. Gen. Schiess
summary: The White House's nominee to succeed Chief of Space Operations Gen. B. Chance Saltzman prioritizes orbital warfare systems. Lt. Gen. Schiess Fielding emphasizes the need for more such systems.

title: Sateliot and Telefónica España to Explore 5G Satellite Connectivity for Defense, Military Uses
summary: Sateliot has partnered with Telefónica España to explore 5G satellite connectivity for defense, military, and remote area applications. The collaboration aims to enhance Telefónica's network coverage using Sateliot's satellite IoT technology.

title: Lite Coms USG Sales Director George Cerasani Talks Network Resiliency at Modern Day Marine
summary: Lite Coms USG Sales Director George Cerasani discusses the company's tactical, multi-orbit connectivity solutions at Modern Day Marine. The focus is on network resiliency in tactical communications.

title: Skynopy Signs Frequency Management Partnership with CNES
summary: Skynopy has partnered with CNES to optimize frequency allocation for its ground stations in France. The collaboration aims to enhance frequency management for Skynopy's operations.

title: York Space Systems to Acquire Terminal Developer All.Space in $355M Deal
summary: York Space Systems is acquiring terminal developer All.Space in a $355 million deal to create a complete communications ecosystem. The acquisition aims to enhance York's offerings in the space industry.

title: FCC Updates EPFD Framework that Covers GEO/NGSO Spectrum Sharing
summary: The FCC has adopted a new framework for Equivalent Power Flux Density (EPFD) to govern spectrum-sharing between GEO and NGSO systems. This update addresses longstanding concerns about interference and coexistence in satellite communications.

title: EarthDaily CEO Don Osborne Previews Upcoming Launch in Thursday Morning Conversation
summary: EarthDaily Analytics is preparing for a critical launch of six satellites this quarter, marking its transition into commercial service. CEO Don Osborne discussed this milestone in a recent conversation.

title: Department of Air Force Plans Initiative to Hire AI Talent
summary: The U.S. Department of the Air Force (DAF) announced an initiative to recruit top AI talent, which will impact the government's space strategy. This move aims to enhance technological capabilities in the space sector.

title: Canada Cancels WildFireSat Constellation Contract With Spire
summary: Canada has canceled its contract with Spire Global to build the WildFireSat wildfire-monitoring satellite constellation. However, the government still plans to proceed with the space-based wildfire monitoring program.
